content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of the integers 111 to NNN, and an integer KKK.

For this permutation PPP, let GPG_PGP​ denote the undirected graph on NNN vertices, which has edges between iii and PiP_iPi​ for each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N (and no other edges).

It is well-known that each connected component of GPG_PGP​ is a cycle.

For each x=1,2,…,Nx = 1, 2, \ldots, Nx=1,2,…,N, solve the following problem **independently**:


You are allowed to choose any two indices iii and jjj, and swap PiP_iPi​ and PjP_jPj​.

Find the minimum number of swaps needed so that the component of GPG_PGP​ containing xxx is a cycle of size **exactly** KKK.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and KKK — the number of elements in AAA and the desired cycle size.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ denoting the initial permutation PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers. The iii-th of them should denote the minimum number of swaps needed such that iii is part of a size-KKK cycle.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4⋅1054 \cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: 111 is already in a cycle of size 111.
Test case 222: Neither 111 nor 222 are in cycles of size 222.
Swapping P1P_1P1​ and P2P_2P2​ puts them both in a cycle of size 222.
Test case 333: 1,5,6,81, 5, 6, 81,5,6,8 are already in a length-444 cycle.
Swapping P2P_2P2​ and P3P_3P3​ puts all of 2,3,4,72, 3, 4, 72,3,4,7 in a length-444 cycle, so all their answers are 111.
